# Wheeler Ecosystem — Phase 11: Observability Performance Plan

**Date:** 2026-05-23
**Status:** READ-ONLY AUDIT — Configurations generated, NOT applied
**Generated by:** Principal Infrastructure Optimization Engineer
**Scope:** 3-server Wheeler ecosystem (AIOPS, COREDB, EDGE)

---

## 1. Executive Summary

This audit covers 7 monitoring components across 3 servers. AIOPS hosts the full stack (Prometheus, Loki, Grafana, Netdata, UptimeKuma, ClickHouse, Promtail). COREDB was found with **all monitoring components down** — a critical gap. EDGE runs only Netdata and Node Exporter with **concerning disk pressure** (62% used, dominated by large log files).

### Top-3 Critical Findings

| # | Severity | Finding |
|---|----------|---------|
| 1 | **CRITICAL** | COREDB has ZERO monitoring running — Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and UptimeKuma all stopped |
| 2 | **HIGH** | Prometheus on AIOPS has **no retention configured** (`--storage.tsdb.retention.time` absent), relying on default 15-day retention |
| 3 | **HIGH** | EDGE server at 62% disk with 852MB journald, 331MB syslog, and 100MB+ per-app logs — no log rotation on PM2/application logs |

---

## 2. Server-by-Server Audit

### 2.1 AIOPS (5.78.140.118) — Primary Monitoring Hub

**Profile:** 16 cores, 30GB RAM, 338GB disk (52GB used, 17%)
**Uptime:** 14 days
**Docker Compose stack:** `/root/infrastructure/enterprise/phase2-observability/observability-stack.yml`

#### 2.1.1 Prometheus

| Attribute | Value | Assessment |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Image | `prom/prometheus:latest` | |
| Uptime | 22 minutes (just restarted) | Recent restart cleared WAL; cold cache |
| Storage path | `/prometheus` (Docker volume: `monitoring_prometheus-data`) | |
| Storage size | **195.7 MB** | Very small — only 22 min of data post-restart |
| Series count | **3,090** | Healthy — well within safe range |
| Label pairs | 1,066 | Healthy |
| Chunk count | 6,873 | Healthy |
| Memory (RSS) | **47.1 MB** | Very low — cold cache after restart |
| CPU | 0.00% | Idle |
| Retention | **NOT SET** — default 15 days | **Missing configuration** |
| Scrape interval | 15s (local), 30s (remote) | Reasonable |
| Targets | 4 active (all UP) | |
| Target: hetzner-aiops | localhost:9090 | Self-scraping |
| Target: hostinger-node | 100.98.163.17:9100 | Remote node exporter |
| Target: hostinger-services | 100.98.163.17:8002 | Remote agent service |
| Target: hostinger-health | hostinger-health-exporter:9091 | Docker health exporter |

**Config location:** `/opt/apps/monitoring/prometheus.yml` (bind-mounted into container)

**High-cardinality metrics identified:**
- `frgcrm_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket` — **384 series** (le + handler + path labels)
- `prometheus_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket` — 140 series
- `prometheus_http_response_size_bytes_bucket` — 126 series
- `node_cpu_seconds_total` — 64 series (cpu + mode labels)

**Top label cardinality:**
- `location=hostinger` — 2,032 series (dominates)
- `instance=100.98.163.17:9100` — 1,262 series
- `job=hostinger-node` — 1,262 series
- `server=srv1476866` — 1,262 series

**Recommendations:**
- Set `--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d` (current 15d default is insufficient for trend analysis)
- Set `--storage.tsdb.retention.size=5GB` as safety cap
- Drop `server` label from hostinger targets (redundant with instance)
- Consider reducing `frgcrm_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket` bucket count from default 10-15 to 8 targeted quantiles

#### 2.1.2 Loki

| Attribute | Value | Assessment |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Image | `grafana/loki:latest` | |
| Uptime | 4 hours | |
| Config | `/root/infrastructure/enterprise/phase2-observability/loki/loki-config.yml` | |
| Data size | **13.8 MB** (volume: `loki-data`) | Minimal — WAL/active data only |
| Memory (RSS) | **91.6 MB** | Healthy |
| CPU | 1.05% | Light load |
| Retention | **90 days** (`retention_period: 2160h`) | Well-configured |
| Schema | v13 with tsdb index | Modern, good choice |
| Index period | 24h | Standard |
| Ingestion limit | 16 MB/s (burst 32 MB/s) | Reasonable for 3-server ecosystem |
| Chunk target size | 1.5 MB (snappy compressed) | Good balance |
| Max entries per query | 5,000 | Somewhat low for deep debugging |
| Max streams per user | 5,000 | Adequate |
| Results cache | 256 MB embedded | Good |
| Query splitting | 30m intervals, parallelizable | Good for large range queries |

**Log sources via Promtail:**
- Docker container logs (all containers, JSON parsing)
- PM2 application logs
- Nginx access + error logs
- Traefik access logs (JSON)
- Auth log (security auditing)
- Syslog
- AI agent logs (structured JSON with cost/tokens)
- PostgreSQL logs (COREDB)
- Redis logs (COREDB)
- Kernel logs

**Recommendations:**
- Bump `max_entries_limit_per_query` to 10,000 for production debugging
- Add `max_query_length: 721h` (~30 days) to prevent accidental full-range queries
- Consider reducing `retention_period` to 60d if storage becomes constrained
- Memory projection: at current ingestion rate, expect ~500MB-1GB data per month → 90d = ~1.5-3GB

#### 2.1.3 Grafana

| Attribute | Value | Assessment |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Image | `grafana/grafana:latest` | |
| Version | **13.0.1** | Recent release |
| Uptime | 2 days | |
| Data size | **52.2 MB** (volume: `monitoring_grafana-data`) | Healthy |
| Memory (RSS) | **132.8 MB** | Healthy |
| CPU | 0.74% | Light load |

**Datasources (4 configured):**

| Name | Type | URL | Default |
|------|------|-----|---------|
| Prometheus | prometheus | http://prometheus:9090 | Yes |
| Loki | loki | http://loki:3100 | No |
| ClickHouse | grafana-clickhouse-datasource | http://clickhouse:8123 | No |
| PostgreSQL-AIOps | postgres | aiops-postgres:5432 | No |

**Datasource settings:**
- Prometheus: cacheLevel=High, incrementalQuerying=true, timeInterval=15s, queryTimeout=60s
- Loki: maxLines=5000, timeout=60s
- PostgreSQL: maxOpenConns=5, maxIdleConns=2, connMaxLifetime=300s

**Recommendations:**
- Enable Grafana alerting state history (requires DB backend)
- Configure SMTP for alert notifications (currently no SMTP visible)
- Set `max_open_conns` to 0 (unlimited) for SQLite to improve dashboard load times
- Consider setting `alerting.max_attempts = 3` and `alerting.min_interval = 30s`

#### 2.1.4 Netdata

| Attribute | Value | Assessment |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Image | `netdata/netdata` | |
| Uptime | 4 hours | |
| Cache size | **746 MB** (volume: `netdatacache`) | High — dbengine tier retention |
| Memory (RSS) | **76.6 MB** | Healthy |
| CPU | 1.04% | Light |
| Collection interval | **1 second** | Maximum resolution |
| Retention (tier 0) | 2 hours @ 1s = 512MB | |
| Retention (tier 1) | 48 hours @ 1min = 256MB | |
| Total retention budget | ~768 MB | |
| Web port | 19999 (0.0.0.0, filtered to Tailscale + localhost) | Secured by UFW |
| Plugins enabled | proc, diskspace, cgroups, tc, python.d, go.d, apps, slabinfo | Good coverage |
| Plugins disabled | nfacct, ebpf, cups, freeipmi, perf, xenstat | Good culling |

**Alarms:** Not audited (API requires auth; configured via netdata.conf health section)

**Recommendations:**
- Netdata is well-optimized — no changes needed
- The 746MB cache exceeds the configured 512+256=768MB budget slightly — consider bumping tier 0 to 640MB
- Keep disabled plugins as-is to preserve CPU headroom

#### 2.1.5 UptimeKuma

| Attribute | Value | Assessment |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Image | `louislam/uptime-kuma:1` | |
| Uptime | 2 days | |
| Data size | **9.2 MB** (volume: `uptime-kuma` + `monitoring_uptime-kuma-data: 244KB`) | Minimal |
| Memory (RSS) | **115.2 MB** | Moderate |
| CPU | 0.34% | Light |

**Recommendations:**
- Note: two separate UptimeKuma volumes exist (`uptime-kuma` and `monitoring_uptime-kuma-data`) — consolidate to one
- Configure database backups (SQLite under `/app/data/kuma.db`)

#### 2.1.6 ClickHouse

| Attribute | Value | Assessment |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Image | `clickhouse/clickhouse-server:24.3` | |
| Uptime | 2 days | |
| Memory (RSS) | **874.5 MB** | **Highest single consumer** |
| CPU | 4.01% | Moderate |
| Storage size | Not available (volume not inspected) | |

**Recommendations:**
- 874.5 MB memory is significant — configure `max_server_memory_usage` if not set
- Ensure TTL-based partition cleanup is configured for analytics data
- Consider if ClickHouse analytics data overlaps with Prometheus metrics (data duplication)

#### 2.1.9 AIOPS Cost of Observability

| Resource | Usage | % of Total |
|----------|-------|------------|
| Total monitoring memory | ~1,381 MB | **4.6% of 30GB** |
| Total monitoring CPU | ~9.2% across all tools | Low |
| Total monitoring storage | ~1,021 MB | **0.3% of 338GB** |
| Largest consumer (mem) | ClickHouse: 875 MB | 2.8% |
| Largest consumer (storage) | Netdata cache: 746 MB | 0.2% |

**Verdict:** Observability overhead is well within acceptable limits on AIOPS.

---

### 2.2 COREDB (5.78.210.123) — Database Server

**Profile:** 16 cores, 30GB RAM, 338GB disk (6.2GB used, 2%)
**Uptime:** ~2 days (processes started May 21)

#### 2.2.1 CRITICAL: All Monitoring Down

| Component | Status | Evidence |
|-----------|--------|----------|
| Prometheus | **STOPPED** | Was running at PID 66078 (started 00:57), now gone |
| Grafana | **NOT RUNNING** | No process, no port 3000 |
| Loki | **NOT RUNNING** | No process, no port 3100 |
| UptimeKuma | **NOT RUNNING** | No process, no port 3001 |
| Promtail | **NOT RUNNING** | No process |
| Node Exporter | **NOT RUNNING** | No port 9100 |

**Running services (Docker only):**
- wheeler-postgres (postgres:16) — up 6 hours
- wheeler-redis (redis:7) — up 6 hours
- wheeler-minio (minio/minio:latest) — up 6 hours

**Impact:**
- No metrics collection for the Wheeler database tier
- No log aggregation from COREDB to Loki
- No uptime monitoring for COREDB services
- No alerting on Postgres/Redis/Minio health
- **Database performance is unobservable**

**Root Cause Hypothesis:** All Docker containers showing ~6h uptime suggests a Docker daemon or host restart approximately 6 hours ago. The monitoring stack was likely not configured to auto-start (no restart policy or systemd unit).

**Recommendations:**
1. Recrecreate Prometheus config at `/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml` with COREDB-appropriate targets
2. Deploy monitoring stack with `restart: unless-stopped` in Docker Compose
3. Create systemd service to ensure Docker Compose monitoring stack starts on boot

#### 2.2.2 COREDB Log Inventory

| Path | Size | Notes |
|------|------|-------|
| /var/log total | 46 MB | Healthy |
| journald | 26.7 MB | Healthy |

---

### 2.3 EDGE (187.77.148.88) — Edge/Application Server

**Profile:** 8 cores, 31GB RAM, 387GB disk (239GB used, **62%**)
**Uptime:** ~2 days (Netdata process started May 21)
**OS:** Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS

#### 2.3.1 Netdata

| Attribute | Value | Assessment |
|-----------|-------|------------|
| Version | **v1.43.2** | Current |
| Collection interval | **5 seconds** | Good default |
| Cache size | **339 MB** | Moderate |
| Memory (RSS) | 133 MB | Healthy |
| CPU | ~6% (4.6% netdata + 1.4% node_exporter + 5.3% apps.plugin) | Slightly high |
| Alarms | 213 normal, 1 warning, 0 critical | 1 active warning |
| Mirrored host | srv1476866 | Parent streaming? |

**Recommendations:**
- Investigate the 1 warning alarm
- apps.plugin consuming 5.3% CPU is notable — consider increasing `update every` in `[plugin:apps]` from 5 to 10
- Netdata version v1.43.2 is recent and adequate

#### 2.3.2 Node Exporter

| Attribute | Value |
|-----------|-------|
| Binary | `/usr/local/bin/node_exporter` |
| Port | 9100 |
| Metrics exported | **1,833** |
| Uptime | ~2 days |
| Memory | ~23 MB RSS |

**Note:** The 1,833 metrics is a count of lines in the /metrics endpoint, not unique series. This is a standard node_exporter footprint.

#### 2.3.3 EDGE Log Inventory — HIGH CONCERN

| Path | Size | Risk |
|------|------|------|
| /var/log/journal | **852 MB** | **HIGH** — journald not bounded |
| /var/log/syslog | **331 MB** | **HIGH** — no rotation |
| /var/log/canary | **299 MB** | **HIGH** — app logs unrotated |
| /var/log/surplusai.log | **100 MB** | **HIGH** — single file, unrotated |
| /var/log/frgcrm.log | **99 MB** | **HIGH** — single file, unrotated |
| /var/log/pm2 | **64 MB** | Medium — PM2-managed |
| /var/log/kern.log.1 | 40 MB | Medium — rotated |
| /var/log/sysstat | 35 MB | Low — SAR data |
| **journald total** | **843.7 MB** | **CRITICAL** |

**Total log footprint:** ~2+ GB

**Impact:** EDGE is at 62% disk usage (239GB/387GB). While not yet critical, the trend is concerning — ~2GB in logs alone. Without rotation, these files grow unbounded.

**Recommendations:**
1. Implement logrotate for `/var/log/surplusai.log`, `/var/log/frgcrm.log`, `/var/log/canary`
2. Cap journald at 500MB (`SystemMaxUse=500M` in `/etc/systemd/journald.conf`)
3. Reduce syslog retention to 7 days with rotation
4. Configure PM2 log rotation (`pm2 install pm2-logrotate`)

#### 2.3.4 EDGE Cost of Observability

| Resource | Usage | % of Total |
|----------|-------|------------|
| Monitoring memory | ~160 MB (netdata + node_exporter) | **0.5% of 31GB** |
| Monitoring CPU | ~6% | Low |
| Log storage | ~2 GB | **0.5% of 387GB** |

## 3. Cross-Cutting Analysis

### 3.1 Monitoring Coverage Matrix

| Capability | AIOPS | COREDB | EDGE | Status |
|------------|-------|--------|------|--------|
| Metrics (Prometheus) | Running | **DOWN** | N/A (Node Exporter feeds AIOPS) | Partial |
| Logs (Loki/Promtail) | Running | **DOWN** | **DOWN** | Partial |
| Dashboards (Grafana) | Running | **DOWN** | N/A | Partial |
| Real-time (Netdata) | Running | **DOWN** | Running | Partial |
| Uptime (UptimeKuma) | Running | **DOWN** | N/A | Partial |
| Analytics (ClickHouse) | Running | N/A | N/A | OK |
| Alerting (Alertmanager) | Unknown | N/A | N/A | Unknown |

**Overall score: 7/16 components running = 44% coverage**

### 3.2 Metrics Cardinality Assessment

| Risk Level | Metric | Series | Labels Concerned |
|------------|--------|--------|------------------|
| MEDIUM | `frgcrm_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket` | 384 | le(10+) + handler(10+) + path(10+) |
| LOW | `prometheus_http_request_duration_seconds_bucket` | 140 | Standard internal metrics |
| LOW | `prometheus_http_response_size_bytes_bucket` | 126 | Standard internal metrics |
| LOW | `node_cpu_seconds_total` | 64 | cpu(8) + mode(8) |

**Overall cardinality:** 3,090 series is well within safe limits (<10,000). No immediate action needed but growth should be monitored.

### 3.3 Total Observability Cost

| Server | Memory | CPU (approx) | Storage | % of Infra |
|--------|--------|-------------|---------|------------|
| AIOPS | 1,381 MB | 9.2% | 1,021 MB + logs 310 MB | 4.6% mem, 0.4% disk |
| COREDB | 0 MB | 0% | 0 MB | 0% (DOWN) |
| EDGE | 160 MB | 6% | 339 MB + logs 2,000 MB | 0.5% mem, 0.6% disk |
| **TOTAL** | **1,541 MB** | **~15%** | **~3,670 MB** | |

**Verdict:** Observability overhead is reasonable. The bigger problem is **coverage gaps**, not resource consumption.

### 3.4 Architecture Diagram (Logical)

```

    ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
    │                                                             │
    │  ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────┐   ┌──────────┐   ┌────────┐  │
    │  │Prometheus│   │  Grafana │   │   Loki   │   │ClickHs │  │
    │  │  :9090   │   │  :3000   │   │  :3100   │   │ :8123  │  │
    │  └────┬─────┘   └────┬─────┘   └────┬─────┘   └────────┘  │
    │       │              │              │                       │
    │       │         ┌────┴────┐    ┌────┴────┐                  │
    │       │         │Netdata  │    │Promtail │                  │
    │       │         │ :19999  │    │ :9080   │                  │
    │       │         └─────────┘    └─────────┘                  │
    │       │              │              │                       │
    │  ┌────┴──────────────┴──────────────┴───────────┐           │

    │  └───────────────────────────────────────────────┘           │
    └─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
           │                    │
           │ Tailscale mesh     │
    ┌──────┴──────┐    ┌────────┴──────────┐
    │   EDGE      │    │     COREDB        │
    │ 187.77...88 │    │   5.78.210.123    │
    │             │    │                   │
    │ ┌────────┐  │    │  MONITORING:      │
    │ │Netdata │  │    │  ALL DOWN         │
    │ └────────┘  │    │                   │
    │ ┌────────┐  │    │  Postgres :5432   │
    │ │NodeExp │  │    │  Redis    :6379   │
    │ └────────┘  │    │  Minio    :9000   │
    └─────────────┘    └───────────────────┘
```

## 4. Prioritized Remediation Plan

### Phase 11-A: Critical Recovery (Priority 0)

| # | Action | Server | Effort |
|---|--------|--------|--------|
| 1 | Restore Prometheus on COREDB | COREDB | 15 min |
| 2 | Restore Grafana on COREDB | COREDB | 10 min |
| 3 | Restore Loki + Promtail on COREDB | COREDB | 15 min |
| 4 | Restore UptimeKuma on COREDB | COREDB | 5 min |
| 5 | Add `restart: unless-stopped` to all monitoring containers | AIOPS, COREDB | 10 min |
| 6 | Create systemd unit for monitoring stack auto-start | COREDB | 15 min |

### Phase 11-B: Retention and Configuration (Priority 1)

| # | Action | Server | Effort |
|---|--------|--------|--------|
| 7 | Set `--storage.tsdb.retention.time=30d` on AIOPS Prometheus | AIOPS | 5 min |
| 8 | Set `--storage.tsdb.retention.size=5GB` on AIOPS Prometheus | AIOPS | 5 min |
| 9 | Apply optimized Prometheus config (drop `server` label) | AIOPS | 10 min |
| 10 | Apply optimized Loki config (query limits) | AIOPS | 5 min |
| 11 | Apply Grafana performance tuning | AIOPS | 10 min |

### Phase 11-C: Log Management (Priority 2)

| # | Action | Server | Effort |
|---|--------|--------|--------|
| 12 | Cap journald at 500MB | EDGE | 10 min |
| 13 | Configure logrotate for app logs (surplusai, frgcrm, canary) | EDGE | 15 min |
| 14 | Configure PM2 log rotation | EDGE | 5 min |
| 15 | Reduce syslog retention to 7 days | EDGE | 5 min |
| 16 | Tune journald on AIOPS (cap at 200MB) | AIOPS | 5 min |

### Phase 11-D: Optimization (Priority 3)

| # | Action | Server | Effort |
|---|--------|--------|--------|
| 17 | Configure ClickHouse `max_server_memory_usage` | AIOPS | 10 min |
| 18 | Reduce `frgcrm` histogram bucket count | AIOPS | 15 min |
| 19 | Increase apps.plugin interval on EDGE (5s -> 10s) | EDGE | 5 min |
| 20 | Consolidate duplicate UptimeKuma volumes | AIOPS | 10 min |

## 5. Generated Configuration Templates

All templates are at `/root/templates/observability/`:

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `prometheus-optimization.yml` | Optimized Prometheus config with retention, reduced cardinality |
| `loki-optimization.yml` | Optimized Loki config with query limits |
| `grafana-optimization.ini` | Grafana performance tuning settings |
| `logrotate-app-logs.conf` | Log rotation for application logs (EDGE) |
| `journald-optimization.conf` | journald size caps (all servers) |
| `retention-policy-recommendations.md` | Retention policy by component |
| `safe-apply-observability-optimizations.sh` | Apply script with verification |

| Component | Series Budget | Current | % Used |

| Hetzner AIOPS (self) | 1,500 | 1,058 | 71% |
| Hostinger Node | 2,000 | 1,262 | 63% |

| Hostinger Health | 200 | ~9 | 5% |
| **TOTAL** | **~5,000** | **3,090** | **62%** |

Headroom: ~1,900 series before reaching 5,000 recommended cap.
Growth rate projection: ~50-100 series/month → safe for ~19-38 months.

| Memory > 90% | Netdata | **GAP** | Netdata |
| Disk > 85% | Netdata | **GAP** | Netdata |
| Service down | UptimeKuma | **GAP** | **GAP** |
| Log errors | Loki | **GAP** | **GAP** |
| DB connections | Prometheus (hostinger) | **GAP** | N/A |

**Alerting coverage: 33% (6/18 cell gaps)**

## 8. Verification Checklist (Post-Application)

- [ ] Prometheus on COREDB: `curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/status/tsdb`
- [ ] Grafana on COREDB: `curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/health`
- [ ] Loki on COREDB: `curl -s http://localhost:3100/ready`
- [ ] UptimeKuma on COREDB: `curl -s http://localhost:3001`
- [ ] Prometheus retention set: `curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/status/flags | grep retention`
- [ ] Log rotation active on EDGE: `ls -la /var/log/*.gz`
- [ ] journald capped: `journalctl --disk-usage`
- [ ] PM2 logrotate installed: `pm2 list | grep logrotate`
- [ ] Docker restart policy: `docker inspect aiops-prometheus | jq '.[0].HostConfig.RestartPolicy'`
- [ ] COREDB auto-start: `systemctl is-enabled monitoring-stack`

## Appendix A: SSH Command Reference

```bash
# AIOPS
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 root@5.78.140.118

# COREDB
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 root@5.78.210.123

# EDGE
ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 root@187.77.148.88
```

## Appendix B: Quick Health Check Commands

```bash
# Prometheus
curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/status/tsdb | jq '.data.headStats'
curl -s http://localhost:9090/api/v1/targets | jq '.data.activeTargets[] | {job: .labels.job, health: .health}'

# Loki
curl -s http://localhost:3100/ready
curl -s http://localhost:3100/metrics | grep loki_ingester_chunks_created_total

# Grafana
curl -s http://localhost:3000/api/health

# Netdata
curl -s http://localhost:19999/api/v1/info | jq '{version, alarms}'

# Monitoring Docker stats
docker stats --no-stream --format "table {{.Name}}\t{{.CPUPerc}}\t{{.MemUsage}}" | grep -E "prometheus|loki|grafana|netdata|kuma|clickhouse"
```
